sections.paragraph:
μάκελλον, ου, τό (not originally a Lat. word taken into Gk. [as Rob. 109], since it is quotable in Gk. fr. c. 400 b.c. in an ins fr. Epidaurus [GDI III/1, 3325=IG IV2/1, 102, 107; 296; 298; 301 in the form μάκελλον w. the mng. ‘enclosure, grating’]. The sense ‘meat market’ is found for the Lat. macellum Plautus and Terence+ [III/II b.c.]; the earliest Gk. ex. of μ. in this sense is SIG 783 [IG V/2, 268], 45 [I b.c.] where it is masc., μάκελλος, as also schol. on Aristoph., Eq. 137; Vi. Aesopi G 51 P.; cp. macellus in Martial, and Sahidic and Bohairic versions of 1 Cor 10:25. S. Cadbury below 134 n. 2. Elsewh. the word is neut. [so also Peshîttâ and Harclean Syriac] or the gender cannot be determined. μ. may have reëntered H. Gk. in this new sense; so Hahn 249 n. 6. For the view that μ. may be of Semitic origin [AWalde, IndogF 39, 1921, 82; B-D-F §5, 1 app.], though Doric-Ionic acc. to Varro, De Lingua Lat. 5, 146 Goetz-Schoell, s. JSchneider, TW IV 373f.—Plut., Mor. 277d; Cass. Dio 61, 18, 3 τ. ἀγορὰν τῶν ὀψῶν, τὸ μάκελλον; Vita Aesopi G 51 ὁ μάκελλος where pork is for sale; IG V/1, 149; 150; SIG 783, 45 [μάκελλος]; BCH 17, 1893, 261; 20, 1896, 126; PHerm127 [3] verso, 5) meat market, food market (s. the plan of one at Pompeii in AMau, Pompeji2 1908, 90–97, fr. here in Ltzm., Hdb. on 1 Cor 10:25. Also HCadbury, The Macellum of Corinth: JBL 53, ’34, 134–41 w. a Lat. ins found at Corinth containing the word ‘macellum’: Corinth, Results of Excavations VIII/2, ’31, no. 124; 125) τὸ ἐν μ. πωλούμενον ἐσθίειν eat what is sold in the meat market 1 Cor 10:25.—B. 365. DELG. M-M. TW.

Lat. Lat. = Latin

Gk. Gk. = Greek

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

  1. fr. = from

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

GDI GDI = Sammlung der griechischen Dialekt-Inschriften—List 3

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

  1. ex. = example

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

masc. masc. = masculine

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Vi. Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

neut. neut. = neuter (used when “n.” would be ambiguous)

  1. Gk. H. Gk. = Hellenistic Greek

Hahn Hahn = LH., Rom und Romanismus im griechischen-römischen Osten—List 6

IndogF IndogF = Indogermanische Forschungen—List 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

acc. to acc. to = according to

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

BCH BCH = Bulletin de correspondance hellénique—List 6

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

Hdb. Hdb. = Handbuch (in connection with a specific NT book, the ref. is to a commentary in the series Handbuch zum Neuen Testament, founded by HLietzmann)—List 6

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 611
